rationale: The citing paper uses Mittal, Ross, and Tsiros (2002) in hypothesis development, not just as background. It relies on that study's claim that higher self-efficacy (issue capability) increases upside focus and risk taking, and uses this logic to motivate its own moderated and mediated predictions (political identity/SDO x self-efficacy, mediated by upside focus). The paper also follows Mittal et al. in operationalizing self-efficacy via prior performance in its secondary-data studies. These are meaningful conceptual and design links.
relation_description: Han et al. extend Mittal et al.'s self-efficacy/issue-capability logic by introducing political identity and SDO as boundary conditions on when self-efficacy increases financial risk taking. They also carry forward Mittal et al.'s upside-focus mechanism and performance-based self-efficacy operationalization into their own tests.
